title.en: Ensure cron daemon is enabled and active

prose.en: Ensure cron daemon is enabled and active

description.en: The `cron` daemon is used to execute batch jobs on the system.

rationale.en: While there may not be user jobs that need to be run on the system, the system does have maintenance jobs that may include security monitoring that have to run, and `cron` is used to execute them.

audit.en:
**\- IF \-** cron is installed on the system:  

Run the following commands to unmask, enable, and start `cron`:

```
# systemctl unmask "$(systemctl list-unit-files | awk '$1~/^crond?\.service/{print $1}')"
# systemctl --now enable "$(systemctl list-unit-files | awk '$1~/^crond?\.service/{print $1}')"
```

remediation.en:
**\- IF \-** cron is installed on the system:  

Run the following commands to unmask, enable, and start `cron`:

```
# systemctl unmask "$(systemctl list-unit-files | awk '$1~/^crond?\.service/{print $1}')"
# systemctl --now enable "$(systemctl list-unit-files | awk '$1~/^crond?\.service/{print $1}')"
```

references: NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5: CM-1, CM-2, CM-6, CM-7, IA-5
